contraindications.clinicalRationale: Diuretics can precipitate acute kidney injury (AKI) and electrolyte abnormalities, particularly in patients with low eGFR or concurrent medications that may contribute to kidney hypoperfusion
contraindications.riskDescription: Precipitation of acute kidney injury and electrolyte abnormalities
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Monitor renal function closely, consider dose reduction, review volume status
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Diuretics can precipitate acute kidney injury (AKI) and electrolyte abnormalities, and the hypokalemic response to diuretics is diminished with low eGFR

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Potassium-sparing diuretics may contribute to hyperkalemia in patients with advanced CKD or when used with salt substitutes containing potassium chloride
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Use with caution, monitor potassium levels, consider alternative diuretic types
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Salt substitutes containing potassium chloride should be used with caution in patients with advanced CKD or those taking potassium-sparing diuretics, as they may contribute to hyperkalemia

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Concurrent diuretic use increases risk of volume depletion and AKI, particularly in patients with tenuous volume status when starting SGLT2 inhibitors
contraindications.riskDescription: Volume depletion and increased acute kidney injury risk
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider decreasing diuretic dosages before SGLT2i initiation, monitor volume status
contraindications.sourceSnippet: concern for volume depletion and AKI, particularly among patients treated concurrently with diuretics or who have tenuous volume status

warnings.warningText: Monitor serum creatinine and potassium within 2-4 weeks after starting or changing dose when used with ACEi or ARB
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients on ACE inhibitors or ARBs
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor serum creatinine and potassium within 2-4 weeks, discontinue or reduce dose if >30% increase in creatinine or hyperkalemia develops

warnings.warningText: Consider decreasing thiazide diuretic dosages before commencement of SGLT2i treatment in patients at risk for hypovolemia
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients at risk for hypovolemia starting SGLT2 inhibitors
warnings.actionRequired: Decrease diuretic dose before SGLT2i initiation, advise patients about volume depletion symptoms, follow up on volume status

warnings.warningText: Consider decreasing loop diuretic dosages before commencement of SGLT2i treatment in patients at risk for hypovolemia
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients at risk for hypovolemia starting SGLT2 inhibitors
warnings.actionRequired: Decrease diuretic dose before SGLT2i initiation, advise patients about volume depletion symptoms, follow up on volume status

warnings.warningText: Hypokalemic response to diuretics is diminished with low eGFR and depends on the type of diuretic used
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor potassium levels closely in patients with reduced kidney function, adjust expectations for potassium-lowering effect
